dealSummary: Cursor, an AI coding startup, is raising a $2 billion round from investors including Andreessen Horowitz and Thrive at a $50 billion valuation, according to reports.
Recursive Superintelligence, the developer of a self-teaching AI system, secured at least $500 million in a round led by GV at a $4 billion valuation, the Financial Times reported.
Prediction market platform operator Polymarket is in talks to raise $400 million at a $15 billion valuation, The Information reported.
UK-based CuspAI, which develops AI for materials discovery, is in talks to secure a $200 million round at a valuation over $2 billion, Bloomberg reported.
Netherlands-based chip startup Euclyd is in talks to raise a €100 million round, CNBC reported.
Rivan, a London-based synthetic fuel producer, received £25 million in a round led by IQ Capital.
Swiss advanced manufacturing fermentation startup Planetary secured almost €17 million for its Series A led by Radikal Capital.
